implementations.title: Deterministic max-domain then max-degree
implementations.reason: Select vertex with largest domain size (and degree as tie-breaker) to reduce branching. Deterministic choice avoids randomness overhead and focuses on hardest constraints first.

implementations.title: Deterministic max-domain then max-degree for rollback
implementations.reason: Uncolour vertices with largest domain and degree to free up more constrained colors. Deterministic to reduce randomness overhead.

implementations.title: Deterministic prefer remembered color
implementations.reason: Always try to reuse the last color to maintain consistency and reduce search space. No shuffle to avoid randomness overhead.

implementations.title: Simple greedy by degree order
implementations.reason: A straightforward greedy that orders vertices by degree descending and assigns the smallest available color. This is fast and often gives a decent initial solution, which can be improved by the FCNS search.

implementations.title: Deterministic min-domain max-degree uncoloured vertex selection
implementations.reason: Select the uncoloured vertex with the largest domain size (most constrained) and tie-break by highest degree to reduce branching. This deterministic heuristic often finds better solutions than random selection.

implementations.title: Select highest domain then highest degree for rollback
implementations.reason: When uncolouring, prefer vertices with large domain (more flexibility) and high degree (more constraints) to increase chances of finding a new colour later.

implementations.title: Deterministic colour selection preferring different from remembered
implementations.reason: Always try a colour different from the remembered one to encourage exploration, then fallback to remembered. This avoids randomness and often leads to better solutions.

implementations.title: Simple greedy by degree order
implementations.reason: A straightforward greedy algorithm that orders vertices by degree descending and assigns the smallest available colour. This is fast and often produces a good initial solution, which can be further improved by FCNS.

implementations.title: Prioritize high domain size and high degree for uncoloured vertex
implementations.reason: Selecting vertices with larger domain size and higher degree first can reduce branching and improve search efficiency.

implementations.title: Prioritize high domain size and high degree for rollback vertex
implementations.reason: Selecting vertices with larger domain size and higher degree for rollback can help escape local optima more effectively.

implementations.title: Keep baseline colour selection
implementations.reason: Baseline colour selection is already effective; no improvement found.

implementations.title: Simple greedy by degree order
implementations.reason: A simpler greedy that orders vertices by degree descending and assigns the smallest available color. This is a common baseline that can be improved by more sophisticated heuristics.

implementations.title: Select most constrained uncolored vertex
implementations.reason: Choosing the vertex with smallest domain size (most constrained) reduces branching and improves search efficiency.

implementations.title: Select vertex with highest uncolored degree among candidates
implementations.reason: Among vertices with large domain, picking one with many uncolored neighbors helps propagate constraints and escape dead ends faster.

implementations.title: Select color with fewest conflicts among neighbors
implementations.reason: Choosing a color that minimizes immediate conflicts reduces the chance of future backtracking and improves solution quality.

implementations.title: DSATUR greedy initial coloring
implementations.reason: DSATUR heuristic (largest saturation degree) often produces better initial solutions than the baseline, reducing the number of colors needed.

implementations.title: Select vertex with largest domain and highest degree
implementations.reason: Choosing vertices with larger domain sizes and higher degrees tends to reduce branching and improve constraint propagation.

implementations.title: Select colored vertex with largest domain and highest degree
implementations.reason: Uncoloring vertices with larger domains and higher degrees may free more constraints and help escape dead ends.

implementations.title: Select color minimizing conflicts with colored neighbors
implementations.reason: Minimizing immediate conflicts reduces the chance of creating a dead end and improves search stability.

implementations.title: Greedy by degree with sequential coloring
implementations.reason: A simple greedy algorithm that orders vertices by degree and assigns the smallest available color often produces a good initial solution quickly.

implementations.title: Select uncoloured vertex with smallest domain size
implementations.reason: Choosing the most constrained vertex (smallest domain) reduces branching and improves search efficiency.

implementations.title: Select rollback vertex with highest uncolored degree
implementations.reason: Uncoloring a vertex with many uncolored neighbors increases flexibility and may help escape dead ends.

implementations.title: Select color with least conflict among neighbors
implementations.reason: Minimizing immediate conflicts reduces the chance of future dead ends and improves solution quality.

implementations.title: DSATUR-based greedy initial solution
implementations.reason: DSATUR often produces better initial colorings than the baseline, giving the solver a better starting point.

implementations.title: Deterministic max-domain then max-degree
implementations.reason: Select vertex with largest domain size (most constrained) and break ties by largest degree to reduce branching.

implementations.title: Deterministic max-domain then max-degree
implementations.reason: Uncolour vertices with largest domain (most constrained) and largest degree to free up many constraints.

implementations.title: Deterministic prefer different color
implementations.reason: Always try a different color first to avoid stagnation, then fallback to remembered.

implementations.title: Greedy by degree descending
implementations.reason: Simple greedy that colors vertices in order of decreasing degree, using the smallest available color. This often gives a good initial solution.

implementations.title: Select uncoloured vertex with highest uncolored degree
implementations.reason: Choosing vertices with more uncolored neighbors increases constraint propagation, leading to earlier detection of conflicts and better pruning.

implementations.title: Select colored vertex with most bad colors to uncolor
implementations.reason: Uncoloring vertices that have many forced bad colors (from singleton neighbors) can free up more options and reduce conflicts, improving search efficiency.

implementations.title: Color selection minimizes bad count
implementations.reason: Choosing a color with fewer forced conflicts (bad count) reduces the chance of future dead ends and improves search stability.

implementations.title: DSATUR greedy initial coloring
implementations.reason: DSATUR heuristic (largest saturation degree) often produces better initial colorings than the baseline, reducing the number of colors needed and providing a stronger starting point for FCNS.

implementations.title: Most constrained vertex selection
implementations.reason: Selecting the vertex with the smallest domain size (most constrained) reduces branching and focuses search on hard-to-color vertices, improving efficiency.

implementations.title: Uncolored degree tie-breaker for rollback
implementations.reason: When uncoloring, selecting vertices with higher uncolored degree (more uncolored neighbors) can free more constraints and improve search diversification.

implementations.title: Least conflicting color selection
implementations.reason: Choosing the color that minimizes conflicts with already colored neighbors reduces future backtracking and improves solution quality.

implementations.title: Simple greedy by degree
implementations.reason: A straightforward greedy algorithm that colors vertices in order of decreasing degree, using the first available color. This is fast and often provides a good initial solution for further optimization.

implementations.title: Most constrained vertex selection
implementations.reason: Selecting the vertex with smallest domain size (most constrained) reduces branching and focuses search on hardest subproblems, improving solution quality.

implementations.title: Uncolour most flexible vertices
implementations.reason: Uncolouring vertices with large domain size (most flexible) gives more room for reassignment, while high degree increases impact on neighbours, improving search diversification.

implementations.title: Conflict-minimizing color selection
implementations.reason: Choosing a color that minimizes future conflicts (forbidden counts) for uncolored neighbors reduces the chance of dead ends, improving search efficiency.

implementations.title: DSATUR-based greedy initial solution
implementations.reason: DSATUR heuristic (saturation degree) is a classic and effective greedy method for graph coloring, often producing fewer colors than the baseline, giving a better starting point for FCNS.

implementations.title: Minimum domain heuristic for uncoloured vertex selection
implementations.reason: Selecting vertices with smallest domain size (most constrained) first reduces branching and improves search efficiency. This is a classic heuristic in constraint satisfaction.

implementations.title: Uncolored degree tie-breaker for rollback vertex selection
implementations.reason: When uncolouring vertices, choosing those with many uncolored neighbours frees more constraints and helps escape dead ends more effectively.

implementations.title: Minimum conflict colour selection with memory preference
implementations.reason: Choosing colours that cause fewest conflicts with already coloured neighbours reduces future backtracking. The remembered colour is used as a tie-breaker to maintain stability.

implementations.title: DSATUR-based greedy initial coloring
implementations.reason: DSATUR (saturation degree) heuristic is a classic and effective greedy algorithm for graph coloring. It selects vertices with the most different colors already used by neighbours, which tends to produce a more balanced and lower-color initial solution.

implementations.title: Most constrained vertex selection
implementations.reason: Selecting the vertex with smallest domain (most constrained) reduces branching and often leads to fewer conflicts. Ties broken by highest degree to prioritize vertices that affect many others.

implementations.title: Largest domain uncolouring
implementations.reason: Uncolouring vertices with large domains gives more flexibility to reassign colours, potentially escaping dead ends more effectively. Ties broken by higher degree to increase impact.

implementations.title: Least conflicting colour selection
implementations.reason: Choosing a colour that is least used among neighbours reduces future conflicts and helps maintain larger domains for neighbours, improving search efficiency.

implementations.title: DSATUR greedy initial solution
implementations.reason: DSATUR (saturation degree) heuristic is a classic and effective greedy algorithm for graph coloring. It selects the vertex with the highest number of different colors among its neighbours, which often leads to fewer colors than the baseline degree-based method.

implementations.title: Minimum domain heuristic for uncoloured vertex selection
implementations.reason: Selecting vertices with smallest domain size (most constrained) reduces branching and improves search efficiency.

implementations.title: Uncolored degree tie-breaker for rollback vertex selection
implementations.reason: When multiple vertices have the same domain size and degree, preferring those with more uncolored neighbors helps to free more constraints and escape dead ends more effectively.

implementations.title: Baseline colour selection
implementations.reason: No improvement found; baseline colour selection is already effective.

implementations.title: DSATUR-based greedy initial solution
implementations.reason: DSATUR heuristic (select vertex with highest saturation degree) typically produces better initial colorings than the baseline, reducing the number of colors needed.

implementations.title: Minimum domain heuristic for uncoloured vertex selection
implementations.reason: Selecting vertices with smallest domain size (most constrained) reduces branching and improves search efficiency.

implementations.title: Uncolored degree tie-breaker for rollback vertex selection
implementations.reason: When uncoloring vertices, picking those with many uncolored neighbors helps free more constraints and reduces future conflicts.

implementations.title: Minimum conflict color selection with remembered preference
implementations.reason: Choosing colors that minimize immediate conflicts reduces the chance of future dead-ends, while still preferring the remembered color to maintain stability.

implementations.title: DSATUR-based greedy initial coloring
implementations.reason: DSATUR heuristic (saturation degree) is a classic and effective greedy method for graph coloring, often producing better initial solutions than the baseline.
